title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Type-System Trace: Backlog Age Capacity
summary: A dashboard shows only queue depth, creates message-ID metric labels, ignores unacknowledged work and oldest age, and responds to memory alarms by adding publishers and increasing prefetch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the backlog age capacity increment in messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.
Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Backlog Age Capacity · Theory Backlog Age Capacity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 4205 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
